sentence: Red, blue and yellow are primary colours.
A paintbrush is used to paint pictures.
A drum makes a loud boom sound.
A story has a beginning, a middle and an end.
A circle can be drawn with a compass.
Green is made by mixing blue and yellow.
Orange is made by mixing red and yellow.
Purple is made by mixing red and blue.
A portrait is a picture of a person.
A landscape painting shows the outdoors.
Texture means how something feels or looks like it feels.
Music has rhythm and melody.
A violin is a string instrument.
A flute is a wind instrument.
Drama means acting out stories.
Warm colours include red, orange and yellow.
Cool colours include blue, green and purple.
Shading makes a drawing look three dimensional.
A collage is made by sticking different materials together.
Perspective makes far away objects look smaller.
A still life painting shows objects like fruit and flowers.
A sculptor uses clay or stone to make art.
A storyboard shows scenes of a story in order.
Abstract art uses shapes and colour, not real objects.
The tempo of music means how fast or slow it is.
A loud sound is called forte in music.
A quiet sound is called piano in music.
Improvisation means performing without a script.
A monologue is when one person speaks alone on stage.
Symmetry in art means both sides look the same.
Artists use light and dark to create contrast.
Typography is the art of arranging letters.
Pointillism uses tiny dots of colour to make a picture.
The horizon line in a landscape is where the sky meets the land.
A chord is when you play three or more notes together.
Renaissance art focused on realism and the human body.
Impressionism is a style that captures light and movement.
A theme in drama is the main idea of a story.
Stop motion animation moves objects frame by frame.
Complementary colours are opposite each other on the colour wheel.
Foreground, middle ground and background show depth in a picture.
A treble clef is used in written music for higher notes.
A bass clef is used for lower notes in written music.
Cubism breaks objects into geometric shapes.
Surrealism creates dreamlike, impossible scenes.
A motif is a repeated element in art or music.
Chiaroscuro is the use of strong light and dark in art.
Pop art uses images from popular culture like adverts.
A pentatonic scale has five notes.
Calligraphy is the art of beautiful handwriting.
Colour mixing is how artists make new colours.
A sketch is a rough drawing done quickly.
An artist uses lines, shapes and colour in their work.
A song has verses and a chorus.
A costume helps an actor become their character.
Printing is making copies of an image using ink.
Weaving is a craft that makes fabric.
Photography is the art of taking pictures.
A mosaic is made of small coloured tiles.
Clay can be shaped and then baked hard in a kiln.
Black and white are not colours on the colour wheel.
Tone in art means how light or dark a colour is.
Pattern in art is a repeated design.
Form in art refers to three dimensional shapes.
Space in art is the area around and within objects.
Rhythm in music is the pattern of long and short sounds.
Harmony is when notes sound good together.
A melody is the main tune of a piece of music.
An ensemble is a group of musicians playing together.
Expression in drama shows the character's feelings.
Mime is acting without using words.
A soliloquy is a speech where a character talks to themselves.
A Greek mask is used in ancient theatre.
Digital art is made using computers or tablets.
Street art is made in public spaces.
A cartoon tells a story with drawings.
Animation is a series of drawings that appear to move.
A gallery is a place to display art.
A museum keeps important art and objects safe.
Ceramics is the art of making objects from clay.
Graffiti is writing or drawing on public surfaces.
Watercolour is a type of paint that is mixed with water.
Pastel is a soft drawing material.
Charcoal is used to make dark, smudgy drawings.
A musical note shows how long a sound lasts.
A crotchet lasts for one beat in music.
A minim lasts for two beats in music.
A semibreve lasts for four beats in music.
Notation is the written language of music.
A scale is a set of musical notes in order.
Major scales sound happy and bright.
Minor scales sound sad and dark.
A conductor leads an orchestra with a baton.
A composer writes original pieces of music.
Beethoven was a famous classical composer.
Da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa.
Van Gogh painted The Starry Night.
Picasso was a founder of Cubism.
Architecture is the art of designing buildings.
Graphic design creates visual communication.
